_description: SC 13G Institutional Holdings scorer. Mild positive/negative signal based on direction of change.
role: analyst reading an SC 13G filed by an institutional investor
context: 13G is a passive filing (vs 13D which is activist). Key signals are: whether this is a new position or amendment, direction of stake change, size of stake, and filer identity (passive index fund vs active conviction manager). 13G signals institutional interest but not direct influence.

filing_type_notes.SC_13G: Initial filing — new position crossing 5% threshold for the first time
filing_type_notes.SC_13G_A: Amendment — updating a previously filed 13G (stake increased, decreased, or approaching threshold)
filing_type_notes.conversion_to_13D: If a 13G filer subsequently files a 13D, this signals escalation to activist intent — flag immediately

new_13G_initial_filing.label: SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE to POSITIVE
new_13G_initial_filing.note: Dependent on filer type — active managers score toward 0.899, passive index funds score toward 0.600

amendment_increased_stake.label: WEAK_POSITIVE to SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE
amendment_increased_stake.condition: 13G/A with higher share count than prior filing
amendment_increased_stake.note: Increase signals continued or growing institutional conviction — magnitude matters

amendment_decreased_stake.label: SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE
amendment_decreased_stake.condition: 13G/A with lower share count than prior filing
amendment_decreased_stake.note: Reduction signals waning conviction — size of reduction determines severity

crossed_below_5pct_threshold.label: MILDLY_NEGATIVE
crossed_below_5pct_threshold.condition: 13G/A where reported stake drops below 5% — filer exits mandatory reporting threshold
crossed_below_5pct_threshold.note: Institutional exit below reporting floor is a meaningful negative signal

passive_index_fund.adjustment: Reduce score toward NEUTRAL — position driven by automatic rebalancing, not conviction
passive_index_fund.rationale: Index funds must hold any stock in the index regardless of outlook — 13G changes reflect index composition, not investment thesis
passive_index_fund.score_guidance: For passive filers, compress score toward 0.000 — e.g. new position scores 0.600 → 0.400, decrease scores -0.200 → -0.100

active_manager.adjustment: Keep score as-is or apply slight upward bias — position reflects deliberate investment thesis
active_manager.rationale: Active managers make discretionary buy/sell decisions — 13G changes carry informational content
active_manager.score_guidance: Maintain full score or add up to +0.050 for highly regarded active managers with strong track records

escalation_flag.trigger: 13G filer subsequently files SC 13D on the same issuer
escalation_flag.action: Flag in master_summary as '13G_to_13D_escalation' — reclassify signal as activist entry
escalation_flag.score_impact: Escalation itself is a strong positive catalyst — treat as new 13D event and score accordingly
escalation_flag.note: The conversion from passive 13G to activist 13D is one of the strongest institutional signals available

mandatory_range.note: 13G is NEVER strongly negative (institutional exits are gradual) and NEVER strongly positive (passive filing lacks activist force). Hard boundaries on both sides.

tasks: Identify the filer name and classify as passive_index_fund or active_manager
Determine filing type: new SC 13G (initial) or SC 13G/A (amendment)
For amendments: compare current stake to prior filing stake — determine direction (increase, decrease, or below 5%)
Select base score range from scoring_guide based on filing type and direction
Apply filer_type_modifier: compress toward neutral for passive index funds, maintain for active managers
Check master_summary: has this filer previously filed a 13G on this issuer? Note trend.
Write a 1-sentence summary identifying filer, stake size, and direction of change
Write a short forecast (1-3 sentences) on implications for institutional ownership trend
Update master_summary with filer, filing date, stake %, direction, and filer type
Flag escalation_flag in master_summary if any subsequent 13D filing is detected from the same filer

output_schema.score: float in [-0.500, +0.899] — mild signal in either direction
output_schema.label: string — sentiment label matching score tier
output_schema.summary: string — 1-sentence description of filer, stake, and change direction
output_schema.forecast: string — 1-3 sentence outlook on institutional ownership trend and implications
output_schema.filer_name: string — name of the institutional investor
output_schema.filer_type_modifier_applied: string — description of adjustment made for filer type
output_schema.master_summary_update: object — fields to append/update in master_summary

rules: mandatory_range: score must always fall within [-0.500, +0.899] — never outside these bounds
passive index funds (Vanguard, BlackRock, State Street, etc.) must have scores compressed toward 0.000 — their holdings reflect index membership, not conviction
active managers (Fidelity, T. Rowe, Wellington, Soros, etc.) carry full conviction signal — maintain or slightly boost score
new 13G initial filing is more meaningful than an amendment increase — slightly higher base score
crossing below 5% threshold is the most negative 13G signal — score toward -0.500
13G→13D escalation must be flagged in master_summary and treated as a new, separate 13D scoring event
always record direction of change (increase/decrease) relative to prior 13G/A in master_summary
if no prior 13G exists in master_summary for this filer on this issuer, treat as new position
